# Daily Brief: 2026-04-30 (Thursday)

## Coach commentary

Day A push went down yesterday with the planned weights and the run, so today is rest, full stop. HRV at 32 against your 7-day average of 29 says recovery is doing its job. Yesterday's macros came in tight (1,612 cal, 156g protein, 3g over the Phase 2 floor of 150g) which is the right outcome on a training day. The scale is showing 204.7 this morning, putting you at 10.3 lb down since the cut started on April 15. That's mostly water through Phase 1, but the rate is exactly on plan.

## Where you stand

**Last-night sleep:**

| Metric | Last Night | 7-Day Avg |
|---|---|---|
| Sleep score | 75 | 71 |
| HRV overnight | 32 ms | 29 ms |
| RHR | 58 bpm | 59 bpm |
| Body battery start | 41 | 38 |
| Training readiness | 76 (moderate) | 68 |

**Weight trend (Garmin source):**

| Date | Weight (lb) |
|---|---|
| 2026-04-15 (cut start) | 215.0 |
| 2026-04-22 (day 8) | 209.4 |
| 2026-04-29 (day 15) | 205.2 |
| 2026-04-30 (day 16) | 204.7 |

## Today's plan

### Training

**Recommendation: rest day.**

Training log shows Day A completed yesterday, 2026-04-29. Per the rotation, rest day follows. Tomorrow is Day B (Upper Pull + Run).

Recovery priorities: 20-30 minute walk, hydration to 80 oz minimum, sauna at Lifetime if you go.

### Diet

**Phase 2, day 16 of cut. Protein floor: 150g/day.**

MFP goals show 1,750 calories and 150g protein, matching Phase 2 target.

**Yesterday in review (2026-04-29):** 1,612 cal against 1,750 target, 156g protein against 150 floor. Solid execution.

**Today in progress (as of 9:42 AM):** Nothing logged yet. Eating window opens at noon. You have until 8 PM to land 150g protein.

### Calendar

Workout window 10:00-11:30 AM is clear today, but rest day so irrelevant. Two meetings 1-2 PM and 3-4 PM; eat lunch before 1 PM to avoid getting derailed.

## Open flags

No flags fired. Carry on the plan.

---

*Generated by fitness coach at 2026-04-30T09:42. Sources: Garmin daily through 2026-04-30, MFP through 2026-04-30T09:30, calendar, coaching docs.*
